title: How to hide formula errors (show 0 or blank instead of #DIV/0!, #N/A)
task: Replace ugly error values like #DIV/0! or #N/A with a 0, a blank, or a friendly message.

excel.note: Return 0 on any error. Use "" for blank, or "Not found" for text.

explanation: IFERROR(value, value_if_error) returns the second argument whenever the first would be any error. 10/0 is #DIV/0!, so this returns 0. Use IFNA() if you only want to trap #N/A and still see other errors.
